name: Curried Shrimp Topping
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup finely diced red bell pepper
1 tablespoon orange all-fruit fruit spread (or orange marmalade)
8 ounces medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, heat oil. Add onion, celery, pepper, and garlic; sauté until crisp-tender, about 4 to 5 minutes. Stir in curry powder; cook for an additional minute. Add broth, raisins, and fruit spread. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes. Add shrimp and cook for 2 more minutes.
2. In a small bowl, combine half-and-half with cornstarch and salt. Stir into the shrimp mixture in the skillet. Cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the mixture boils and thickens.
3. Cut potatoes lengthwise, stopping just below the base of the potato. Mash slightly with a fork, leaving them mostly intact. Spoon the curried shrimp topping over each potato, dividing evenly. Garnish with cucumber and mint.
Makes 4 servings. Preparation time: About 25 minutes. Cooking time: About 20 minutes.
Per serving: Approximately 336 calories, 19 grams protein, 56 grams carbohydrates, 6 grams fat, 16% of calories from fat, 93 milligrams cholesterol, 674 milligrams sodium, and 6 grams fiber.
Busted by Gail Shermeyer
